title: Strategy Architect Skills — Options Strategies & Strike Selection
description: Knowledge base for the Strategy Architect: mapping market regimes to optimal options strategies, defining the physical construction of spreads, and applying Greek-based strike selection guardrails. Prioritizes theta-decay and premium collection.

credit_spreads.name: Credit Spreads (Bull Put / Bear Call)
credit_spreads.construction: Sell an Out-of-the-Money (OTM) option and Buy a further OTM option of the same type for protection.
credit_spreads.goal: Collect net premium. You want the market to stay away from your short strike.
credit_spreads.greeks: Positive Theta (time is your friend). Directional Delta (Bull Put = Positive Delta, Bear Call = Negative Delta).

debit_spreads.name: Debit Spreads (Bull Call / Bear Put)
debit_spreads.construction: Buy an At-the-Money (ATM) or slightly OTM option and Sell a further OTM option.
debit_spreads.goal: Pay a net debit to capture a strong directional move.
debit_spreads.varaha_rule: Avoid Debit Spreads unless the Scout reports an 'Extreme Trend' (ADX > 50) where theta decay is outweighed by massive directional momentum.

iron_condor.name: Iron Condor
iron_condor.construction: Combine a Bull Put spread and a Bear Call spread. Sell OTM Call + Sell OTM Put, protected by buying further OTM wings.
iron_condor.goal: Capture theta decay in a range-bound market.
iron_condor.greeks: Highly Positive Theta. Delta Neutral. Negative Vega (benefits from volatility dropping).

iron_butterfly.name: Iron Butterfly (Iron Fly)
iron_butterfly.construction: Sell the ATM Call + Sell the ATM Put (a short straddle), protected by buying OTM Call and OTM Put wings.
iron_butterfly.goal: Maximum theta collection and vega crush. Extremely narrow profit tent.
iron_butterfly.greeks: Highest Positive Theta. Delta Neutral. Highly Negative Vega.

ratio_spreads.name: Ratio Spreads (Front Ratio)
ratio_spreads.construction: Buy 1 ATM option, Sell 2 OTM options (e.g., Buy 1 24000 CE, Sell 2 24200 CE).
ratio_spreads.goal: Finance a directional trade by selling extra premium. Creates a 'trap' where maximum profit is achieved if the price pins exactly at the short strikes.
ratio_spreads.risk: Has naked risk on one side if the market blows past the short strikes. Requires strict Portfolio Manager oversight.

calendar_spread.name: Calendar Spread (Horizontal / Time Spread)
calendar_spread.construction: SELL a near-term option + BUY a far-term option at the SAME strike. Uses TWO different expiries (e.g., SELL May 14 weekly CE + BUY May 21 weekly CE at strike 23650).
calendar_spread.goal: Achieve DOUBLE-POSITIVE Greeks: positive Theta (near-term decays faster) AND positive Vega (far-term is more IV-sensitive).
calendar_spread.greeks: POSITIVE Theta (short near-term decay > long far-term decay). POSITIVE Vega (far-term vega > near-term vega). Delta near neutral if ATM.
calendar_spread.expiry_rule: Librarian MUST resolve TWO different expiries for the SAME strike. Near-term = this week (for SELL), far-term = next week (for BUY). If only one expiry available in scrip_master, flag it.
calendar_spread.risk: Profits if spot stays near strike and near-term decays faster. Loses if spot moves far from strike or if IV drops (vega loss on far-term). Theta decays both legs — the net decay rate difference is the edge.
calendar_spread.varaha_rule: Only deploy when VIX is elevated (VIX > 16) and spot is near a round strike. Theta edge shrinks when both expiries are far out.

sideways.logic: The market is chopping within a range. Sell the 0.15 Delta strikes on both sides. Let theta decay work its magic safely away from the current spot price.

choppy.logic: High VIX means premiums are inflated, but ADX < 25 means there is no trend. Sell the ATM straddle to capture massive premium and wait for the 'Vega Crush' when VIX inevitably drops.

trending_bull.logic: The trend is up. Sell puts below the market to collect premium, letting the upward momentum and theta decay work together.

trending_bear.logic: The trend is down. Sell calls above the market to generate safe yield as the market falls away from your strikes.

double_positive.condition: User explicitly requests both Vega-positive and Theta-positive simultaneously
double_positive.logic: SELL near-weekly ATM + BUY far-weekly ATM at the SAME strike. Near-term Theta (positive) decays faster than far-term Theta (negative) bleeds. Far-term Vega (positive) dominates near-term Vega (negative). Requires TWO different expiries. This is the ONLY standard spread that achieves net-positive Vega AND net-positive Theta.
double_positive.secondary_strategy: None — calendar is the only double-positive structure in standard options.
double_positive.avoid: All single-expiry spreads (Iron Condor, Iron Butterfly, Credit/Debit Spreads) — they are always vega/theta inverse.

delta_rule.name: The 0.15 Delta Rule
delta_rule.rule: For Iron Condors and Credit Spreads, the short leg must always target a Delta between 0.15 and 0.20. Never sell ATM strikes unless executing an Iron Fly.

wing_width_sizing.name: Wing Width (Risk Sizing)
wing_width_sizing.rule: The protective wings (the bought options) must be placed at a distance greater than the daily_range_estimate calculated by the VIX. For example, if VIX implies a 100-point daily move, the wings must be at least 150 points wide.

vega_check.name: Vega Check
vega_check.rule: Never deploy an Iron Butterfly if VIX is below 12. You will not collect enough premium to justify the risk of the narrow profit tent.
